# Wixie Briefing — elevated patterns

## 22 elevated pattern(s)

### RV01 — Permission rubber-stamp: 93% approval rate on tool-permission prompts

- **Weight:** 0.699   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 2 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-27 (16d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, rubber-stamp, permissions`

**Signal:** A destructive command is approved within <2s of the prompt appearing.

**Counter:** Treat permission prompts as advisory, not load-bearing; require an additional confirmation for destructive verbs regardless of prompt approval. Inject context (lint, diff scope) into the prompt rather than relying on user attention.

---

### MT01 — Defense-in-depth omission for prompt injection

- **Weight:** 0.699   **Posterior:** 0.833 (95% CI [0.4782, 0.9949])   **LLR:** 7.17
- **Observations:** 4 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-27 (16d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, defense-in-depth, prompt-injection`

**Signal:** Threat model lists exactly one mitigation against prompt injection.

**Counter:** Stack at least three layers: capability partition, input rail (classifier or NeMo-style), and output rail (egress monitor).

---

### RV02 — Phantom-diff visual inflation

- **Weight:** 0.699   **Posterior:** 0.800 (95% CI [0.3976, 0.9937])   **LLR:** 5.38
- **Observations:** 3 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-27 (16d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, phantom-diff, visual-inflation`

**Signal:** Diff hunk count or line delta exceeds the actual edit by more than 100x.

**Counter:** Strip whitespace-only and line-ending churn from displayed diffs; show semantic-edit count alongside raw line count.

---

### OP06 — Arxiv abstract URLs (/abs/<id>) are HTML-fetchable; PDFs (/pdf/<id>) return binary unfetchable

- **Weight:** 0.671   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `wixie, deep-research, fetcher, arxiv, url-routing, page-shape`

**Signal:** Before launching a fetcher seed query against an arxiv URL, inspect the path: if it ends in /pdf/<id> or .pdf, rewrite to /abs/<id> or the ar5iv HTML mirror. If a fetcher already returned 'error: unfetchable' on a /pdf/ URL, that is the F08 tool-mis-invocation signature — re-dispatch with the abstract URL.

**Counter:** When constructing a fetcher seed query that targets an arxiv paper, route to the abstract page or the ar5iv HTML mirror, not the PDF. Specifically: prefer https://arxiv.org/abs/<id> for the abstract landing, https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/<id> for full-text HTML, and https://arxiv.org/html/<id>vN for newer papers. Reject https://arxiv.org/pdf/<id> as a fetcher target — WebFetch cannot parse PDF binaries, and the contract requires page-body verbatim quotes which are unreachable from the binary.

---

### MT02 — Skipped lifecycle stage: ship without converge or harden

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, lifecycle, skipped-stage`

**Signal:** A prompt is marked DEPLOY with metadata.json from a prior session and no test/harden artifacts in the current run.

**Counter:** DEPLOY verdict requires all gates passed in the current run, not stored metadata; refuse ship-claim without fresh test + harden artifacts.

---

### RV02 — Phantom-diff UI: small edit appears as full-rewrite diff, breaking review

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.833 (95% CI [0.4782, 0.9949])   **LLR:** 7.17
- **Observations:** 4 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, phantom-diff, ui`

**Signal:** A single Edit call's displayed diff exceeds 100 lines or 10x the actual changed-line count.

**Counter:** Verify diff size against actual edit-tool reported changes; if displayed diff is >10x edit footprint, halt and ask user; surface raw old_string/new_string when display is suspect.

---

### MT03 — Format-model mismatch: Claude-XML prompt shipped to GPT or o-series

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, format-mismatch, cross-model`

**Signal:** A prompt with Claude XML tags is paired with a non-Claude model ID in metadata.json without a translation artifact.

**Counter:** Always run /translate-prompt when retargeting across families; verify with score-comparison before shipping; never copy a Claude XML prompt into a non-Claude folder unchanged.

### RV04 — Session abandonment on unexpected changes (~10-20%)

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.800 (95% CI [0.3976, 0.9937])   **LLR:** 5.38
- **Observations:** 3 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, session-abandonment, scope-creep`

**Signal:** Diff includes edits to files or symbols not named in the user's request.

**Counter:** Surgical changes only â€” touch only flagged lines; mention out-of-scope observations in reply, do not silently fix; before unscoped edit, ask.

### RV01 — Permission rubber-stamping: user accepts ~93% of permission prompts without review

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.833 (95% CI [0.4782, 0.9949])   **LLR:** 7.17
- **Observations:** 4 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, rubber-stamping, permission-fatigue`

**Signal:** A flow emits >5 permission prompts in <2 minutes; or the same prompt class fires for the Nth time without user pause.

**Counter:** Reserve permission prompts for genuinely ambiguous or destructive ops; bundle related ops into one prompt; emit context (paths, refs, irreversible-yes-no) so the user can decide in one read.

---

### MT04 — Honest-numbers violation: DEPLOY claimed with 7/8 SAT assertions passing

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, honest-numbers, verdict-inflation`

**Signal:** A DEPLOY verdict accompanies an assertion count <8 or any axis below the threshold.

**Counter:** DEPLOY requires all 8 assertions, not 7+high-overall; the verdict is rule-based not vibe-based; refuse to inflate.

---

### MT01 — Sycophancy after user pushback: agent abandons a flagged concern when user says 'great!'

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.800 (95% CI [0.3976, 0.9937])   **LLR:** 5.38
- **Observations:** 3 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, sycophancy, user-pushback`

**Signal:** Agent flagged a concern in turn N, user said 'great' or 'looks good' in turn N+1, agent proceeded without re-stating the concern in turn N+2.

**Counter:** Re-assert flagged concerns explicitly after user pushback; keep an internal concerns-log; require the concern to be addressed on substance, not vibes.

---

### RV03 — No per-hunk accept/discard: review degenerates to accept-all or revert-all

- **Weight:** 0.669   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-25 (17d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, per-hunk, ui`

**Signal:** A multi-hunk change is presented with only accept/reject controls; reviewer accepts to keep the good parts and inherits the bad.

**Counter:** Require per-hunk review for diffs >3 hunks; offer split-commit or partial-stage; never present a multi-hunk change as atomic accept/reject.

---

### OP06 — Agent scratch-simulation scripts left behind in repo-root state/ after convergence run

- **Weight:** 0.624   **Posterior:** 0.917 (95% CI [0.7151, 0.9977])   **LLR:** 10.00
- **Observations:** 10 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `wixie, agent-hygiene, scratch-residue, convergence-engine, enchanter-ai, brand-standard, file-hygiene, operational-discipline`

**Signal:** Untracked .py or .sh file appears at repo root or in wixie/state/ after an agent run and contains an open() call with a hardcoded <repo-root>/ absolute path.

**Counter:** When a subagent writes investigative scripts to probe what-if score responses, write to /tmp/<plugin>-<task>-NNN.py or a plugin-scoped state/<plugin>/scratch/ dir with .gitignore coverage. wixie/state/ is reserved for tracked cross-plugin artifacts (precedent.jsonl, learning-substrate state). Sweep and delete scratch before agent return. If the scratch hardcodes an absolute UNC path, it is definitionally not reusable and therefore always scratch.

---

### RV02 — No per-hunk accept/discard — reviewer must accept or reject the whole change

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.800 (95% CI [0.3976, 0.9937])   **LLR:** 5.38
- **Observations:** 3 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, diff, ui, claude-code`

**Signal:** Review surface exposes only a single accept button for a multi-hunk change and some hunks are style-only while others are semantic.

**Counter:** Require per-hunk granularity in any review surface that writes to files, and when unavailable, have the agent split its own edits into one-purpose-per-edit calls so the reviewer can reject a single intent cleanly.

---

### META01 — Skipped lifecycle — agent ships artifact before running its own verification stages

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.857 (95% CI [0.5407, 0.9958])   **LLR:** 8.96
- **Observations:** 5 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, lifecycle, process-discipline, wixie`

**Signal:** Agent is about to hand over an artifact and at least one declared pre-ship stage (test, review, harden) has not produced an output file.

**Counter:** Run the full declared lifecycle in one pass before handing over; heuristic self-check is a sanity check, not convergence, and reactive fixes count as evidence the lifecycle was skipped.

---

### RV03 — Session abandonment on unexpected changes — 10-20% of sessions end after surprise edit

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.917 (95% CI [0.7151, 0.9977])   **LLR:** 10.00
- **Observations:** 10 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, scope-creep, trust, claude-code`

**Signal:** Agent's diff includes style, import, or formatting changes adjacent to a bug-fix request the user did not mention.

**Counter:** Touch only the flagged lines and match the file's local style; if another issue is noticed, mention it in the reply but do not fix silently, and never bundle a refactor with a bug fix.

---

### TM02 — Permission rubber-stamping — user accepts tool permissions at ~93% rate without review

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.941 (95% CI [0.7941, 0.9984])   **LLR:** 10.00
- **Observations:** 15 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, permissions, rubber-stamp, habituation`

**Signal:** User approval latency on permission prompts has dropped below 2 seconds across the last 10 prompts.

**Counter:** Batch low-risk permissions into scoped allowlists at project setup so every individual prompt is high-salience, and require an explicit diff-of-action for any permission that touches destructive tools.

---

### META04 — Honest-numbers violation — DEPLOY claimed on stale or self-reported scores

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.833 (95% CI [0.4782, 0.9949])   **LLR:** 7.17
- **Observations:** 4 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, honest-numbers, verdict, wixie`

**Signal:** Agent is about to claim a DEPLOY verdict and the scores being cited were produced in a prior session or before the last convergence change.

**Counter:** A shipping verdict comes from the current run's scores and 8/8 SAT assertions; re-run self-eval when unsure, and never claim DEPLOY with stored metadata from a prior session.

---

### META02 — Untagged failure logging — free-text notes that don't compound across sessions

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.875 (95% CI [0.5904, 0.9964])   **LLR:** 10.00
- **Observations:** 6 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, taxonomy, learnings, accumulation`

**Signal:** Failure-log entry is free-text prose without a tag from the declared taxonomy or is missing any of the required fields.

**Counter:** Every failure-log entry tags exactly one canonical code from the declared taxonomy and carries the required fields (code, axis, hypothesis, outcome, counter) so cross-session aggregation can compound.

---

### RV01 — Phantom-diff inflation — 11-line change displayed as +7490/-6880 in the review UI

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.833 (95% CI [0.4782, 0.9949])   **LLR:** 7.17
- **Observations:** 4 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `review, diff, ui, claude-code`

**Signal:** Diff reports thousands of added and deleted lines on a file whose content-level change is under 20 lines.

**Counter:** Strip whitespace-only and EOL-only hunks from the review diff by default, show true-content changes first, and expose a collapsed all-hunks view only on explicit toggle.

---

### META03 — Branding drift — declared algorithm name diverges from implemented behavior

- **Weight:** 0.623   **Posterior:** 0.750 (95% CI [0.2924, 0.9916])   **LLR:** 3.58
- **Observations:** 2 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-22 (20d ago)
- **Tags:** `meta, branding-drift, naming-discipline, enchanter-ai`

**Signal:** A component carries a name like Bayesian, Gauss, or Thompson and grepping its source reveals only a single-line learning-rate update.

**Counter:** Before shipping any engine with a specific-algorithm label, grep the implementation for the corresponding math (prior + posterior update, not just a learning rate) and rename to the honest class (EMA, exponential smoothing) if the math is absent.

---

### F07 — Ran seven reactive iterations instead of one-pass Wixie lifecycle

- **Weight:** 0.603   **Posterior:** 0.889 (95% CI [0.6306, 0.9968])   **LLR:** 10.00
- **Observations:** 7 across 1 session(s)   **Last seen:** 2026-04-21 (22d ago)
- **Tags:** `wixie, lifecycle, convergence, prompt-engineering, process-discipline`

**Signal:** When generating any prompt artifact for Wixie, walk the four lifecycle stages in order before the user sees the result. If you find yourself applying a schema fix because the user flagged a warning, the lifecycle was skipped.

**Counter:** Run the full Wixie lifecycle in one pass before handing over: /create → /converge (Sonnet optimizer + Haiku reviewer, not heuristic loops) → /test-prompt (surfaces tests.json + metadata shape + format/registry mismatch) → /harden (red-team audit on high-stakes prompts) → hand over with honest verdict. Heuristic self-eval is a sanity check, not convergence.
